titleEn: Croplands expands WEED-IT range with new releases, including orchard solution
titleZh: Croplands扩展WEED-IT系列，发布包括果园解决方案在内的新产品
summaryZh: 澳大利亚Croplands Equipment公司发布了WEED-IT精准喷洒系列的多项新技术，包括第二代机器人就绪型Pinto、大型宽幅平台、扩展的改装能力以及首款专为果园设计的光学点喷解决方案。其中Series 2 Robot Ready Pinto结合了SwarmFarm Robotics的SwarmBot、WEED-IT光学点喷技术和成熟的Pinto平台，可实现自动化喷洒作业，提高精度和效率，节省化学品并增加灵活性。
featuredReason: 该新闻展示了精准农业技术在种植业（大田和果园）中的最新进展，特别是光学点喷与自主机器人的结合，对智慧畜牧行业人士理解跨领域自动化技术趋势有参考价值。
contentFull: Buyers guides Field robots Autosteer retrofit kits Autonomous Retrofit Kits Autonomous Tractors Drones Smart weeders Tech in focus Autonomoussemi-auto steering systems Field robots Drones Smart farming Indoor farming Tools & data Sustainability Crop solutions Fertilizing Tillage WeedPest control Irrigation Harvest & storage Digital magazine Newsletter Our sites Subscribe LoginRegister Subscribe Partners Newsletter Subscribe ![Image 2: login!Image 3: logout](https:www.futurefarming.comsmart-farmingcroplands-expands-weed-it-range-with-new-releases-including-orchard-solution) ![Image 4: Future Farming](https:www.futurefarming.com) Buyers guides Field robots Autonomous Tractors Autonomous Retrofit Kits Autosteer Retrofit Kits Drones Smart weeders Tech in focus Drones Field robots Autonomoussemi-auto steering systems Crop solutions Fertilizing Tillage WeedPest control Irrigation Harvest & storage Smart farming Indoor farming Tools & data Sustainability ![Image 5](https:www.futurefarming.comsmart-farmingcroplands-expands-weed-it-range-with-new-releases-including-orchard-solution) Logout Gateway to the world of smart farming Croplands expands WEED-IT range with new releases, including orchard solution 18-06 | Smart farming | News LinkedInFacebookXWhatsAppPrint !Image 6: Combining SwarmFarm Robotics’ SwarmBot, WEED-IT optical spot spraying technology and the proven Pinto platform, the new implement enables growers to automate spraying operations while maximising accuracy and efficiency – delivering chemical savings and greater flexibility in chemistry application. Photo: Croplands Combining SwarmFarm Robotics’ SwarmBot, WEED-IT optical spot spraying technology and the proven Pinto platform, the new implement enables growers to automate spraying operations while maximising accuracy and efficiency – delivering chemical savings and greater flexibility in chemistry application. Photo: Croplands Australian company Croplands Equipment has unveiled a wave of new technology across its WEED-IT precision spraying range, headlined by the Series 2 Robot Ready Pinto and spanning a new large-scale broadacre platform, expanded retrofit capability and the company’s first optical spot spraying solution purpose-built for orchards. Leading the new releases is the Series 2 Robot Ready Pinto, the next generation of Croplands’ autonomous spraying platform. Combining SwarmFarm Robotics’ SwarmBot, WEED-IT optical spot spraying technology and the proven Pinto platform, the new implement enables growers to automate spraying operations while maximising accuracy and efficiency – delivering chemical savings and greater flexibility in chemistry application. The Series 2 brings targeted engineering refinements to the platform, including an upgraded tank design for improved centre of gravity, along with a suite of further enhancements developed for the demands of autonomous field operation. Croplands WEED-IT Product Lead Lachlan Coom says the update reflects both grower feedback and the growing maturity of the autonomous spraying market. “The Series 2 Robot Ready Pinto builds on a platform that growers already trust, with dedicated design enhancements.” New Pegasus platform Alongside the Series 2, Croplands is releasing a purpose-built WEED-IT-ready 36-metre truss boom mounted to its renowned Pegasus 7000L sprayer. The result is a more compact large-scale broadacre sprayer with twin tank and twin spray line capabilities, giving farmers a versatile machine capable of spot spraying, blanket spraying or both simultaneously. The platform has been extensively field tested over the past two years ahead of its release. “We are excited about the launch”, says Coom. “It’s about delivering practical solutions that fit into real farming systems, while giving operators more flexibility in how and when they spray.” Strong momentum is also building around Croplands’ WEED-IT Retrofit Kits, with the company’s engineering and field service teams having successfully installed optical spot spraying sensors across a wide range of sprayer brands. Self-propelled, linkage and trailed units from Case Patriot, John Deere, Goldacres, Fendt Rogator, Hayes and Hardi have all been retrofitted, reinforcing the adaptability of the WEED-IT system. Coom: “We’ve worked on a broad mix of machines and so far, we haven’t come across a sprayer we couldn’t retrofit. Growers can upgrade existing equipment with WEED-IT technology and start seeing savings without needing to invest in a completely new unit.” WEED-IT heading to the orchard Those savings are driven by WEED-IT’s ability to significantly reduce chemical use, improve spraying efficiency and help combat herbicide resistance in hard-to-control weeds. The technology’s credentials are well established: over the past 15 years, Croplands has installed more than 25,000 WEED-IT sensors, primarily in broadacre applications where the system has redefined spraying practices and supported more sustainable farming outcomes. Now, that same capability is being extended to horticulture with the introduction of the WEED-IT Orchard 4000 – the company’s first full-row optical spot spraying solution designed specifically for orchard weed control. The system delivers targeted herbicide application across entire rows, bringing the precision spraying benefits long available to broadacre growers to the tree crop market for the first time. Supporting the full range is the WEED-IT DASH 10″ touchscreen, designed to give operators greater confidence and insight into their spraying performance, with remote upgrade and support capability built in. Croplands will have WEED-IT equipment and technology on display across several upcoming field days in Australia. translationZh:
澳大利亚公司Croplands Equipment在其WEED-IT精准喷洒系列中推出了一系列新技术，主打产品是Series 2 Robot Ready Pinto，此外还包括一个新的宽幅大田平台、扩展的改装能力以及公司首款专为果园设计的光学点喷解决方案。

**Series 2 Robot Ready Pinto**

引领这些新发布的是Series 2 Robot Ready Pinto，这是Croplands自主喷洒平台的下一代产品。该新机具结合了SwarmFarm Robotics的SwarmBot、WEED-IT光学点喷技术和成熟的Pinto平台，使种植者能够实现喷洒作业自动化，同时最大化精度和效率——从而节省化学品并在化学药剂应用方面获得更大的灵活性。

Series 2对平台进行了有针对性的工程改进，包括升级的罐体设计以改善重心，以及一系列针对自主田间作业需求开发的进一步增强功能。

Croplands WEED-IT产品负责人Lachlan Coom表示，此次更新反映了种植者的反馈以及公司对持续改进的承诺。
contentHtml: Australian company Croplands Equipment has unveiled a wave of new technology across its WEED-IT precision spraying range, headlined by the Series 2 Robot Ready Pinto and spanning a new large-scale broadacre platform, expanded retrofit capability and the company’s first optical spot spraying solution purpose-built for orchards.
